content:
First observe that for any odd prime \(p\neq 3\),  
\[S=9+9^2+\cdots+9^{2010}=\frac{9\bigl(9^{2010}-1\bigr)}{8}\equiv0\pmod p\]
if and only if 
\[9^{2010}\equiv1\pmod p,\]
i.e.\ the order of 9 mod \(p\) divides 2010.  Checking the primes in order:

2) \(9\equiv1\pmod2\), so \(2\mid S\).  
3) \(3\mid9\), so \(3\mid S\).  
5) \(9\equiv4\pmod5\), ord\(_5(9)=2\mid2010\), so \(5\mid S\).  
7) \(9\equiv2\pmod7\), ord\(_7(9)=3\mid2010\), so \(7\mid S\).  
11)\(9\pmod{11}\) has order 5 and \(5\mid2010\), so \(11\mid S\).  
13)\(9\pmod{13}\) has order 3 and \(3\mid2010\), so \(13\mid S\).  
17)\(9\pmod{17}\) has order 8, but \(8\nmid2010\), so  
\[9^{2010}\not\equiv1\pmod{17}\quad\Longrightarrow\quad17\nmid S.\]

Hence the smallest prime not dividing \(S\) is
\[
\boxed{17}.
\]

5) \(9\equiv4\pmod5\), ord\(_5(9)=2\mid2010\), so \(5\mid S\).  
7) \(9\equiv2\pmod7\), ord\(_7(9)=3\mid2010\), so \(7\mid S\).

\[
\boxed{17}.
